Name: pac_bayes_spectral_stability
Title: PAC-Bayes Frameworks for Spectral Stability Bounds in Machine Learning Inference
Short Hypothesis: PAC-Bayes frameworks can be used to derive spectral stability bounds for machine learning models, ensuring robust and reliable inference.
Related Work: Existing research has applied PAC-Bayes frameworks to machine learning models, but not to spectral stability bounds. This proposal extends PAC-Bayes to spectral stability bounds, filling a gap in the current literature.
Abstract: This proposal presents a novel approach to deriving spectral stability bounds for machine learning models using PAC-Bayes frameworks. By leveraging PAC-Bayes, we develop robust and reliable models resilient to perturbations and uncertainties. The proposal outlines a research plan to develop and evaluate this approach, focusing on empirical validation, mechanistic transparency, and reproducible designs.
Experiments: Apply the PAC-Bayes framework to a range of machine learning models and tasks
Evaluate the resulting stability bounds using metrics such as robustness and reliability
Compare the performance of the PAC-Bayes framework to existing methods for deriving spectral stability bounds
Risk Factors and Limitations: Computational expense of the PAC-Bayes framework
Need for careful selection of hyperparameters and model architectures
